summary.oneSentence: Tormund Vale is a giant floodplain civilization where lock houses, granaries, relief halls, healing courts, training arenas, memory-tablet government, and the Sky-Stair Hall turn scale into logistics, protection, and perseverance.
summary.identityStatement: Tormund Vale should read as an old river-and-floodplain giant city, not a generic giant fortress. Its civic identity comes from enormous waterworks, riverstone terraces, limewashed civic blocks, reed pavilions, timber longhouses, grain halls, blue order sigils, ward networks, recovery halls, public rosters, giant-scale streets, and memory-tablet authority.

evidence: hero sidecars identify a full civic system with Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, Lock House Beacon, and Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud
street sidecars show complete civic life across canal/riverworks, granary/food market, civic services, medical/healing, and arena/training districts
district anchors emphasize public administration, water gauges, grain scales, public rosters, clinics, recovery beds, and training yards rather than temporary spectacle
the city is built around floodplain and river conditions as permanent civic infrastructure

skyline: Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud as the far wonder-scale anchor
Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry as the dominant central seat of power
Lock House Beacon as the near/front riverworks signal landmark
giant-scale terraces, waterworks, reed pavilions, training grounds, and civic service halls spread across floodplain and canal basin
large public works and registry architecture reading as logistical authority rather than palace display
visualPatterns: giant scale is expressed through civic logistics: enormous gates, halls, scales, basins, rosters, and arenas
water management and food distribution are foundational civic symbols, not background utilities
public care is repeated across relief and healing districts, making recovery part of city identity
training and strength are formalized through arenas and champion gates rather than uncontrolled violence
memory tablet walls connect giant scale to remembrance, registry, and long-term institutional continuity

environmentalRelationship.observed: Tormund Vale treats the floodplain as an engineering and logistics system. The river is not scenery; it is measured, gated, diverted, fed through waterwheels, tied to grain markets, and connected to relief, healing, and training infrastructure.
evidence: Section 1 is Canal / Riverworks with Lock House Beacon, sluice gates, waterwheel yards, and water gauges
Section 2 is Granary / Food Market with grain scales, bread ovens, storage jars, and covered food halls
Section 3 Civic Services uses bread tokens, water basins, blue service lamps, and public rosters
all street sidecars specify Floodplain and River terrain/water context

authorityExpression.observed: Authority is logistical, archival, and provision-based. The city governs by measuring water, feeding people, recording obligations, maintaining relief systems, healing the injured, and training replacements strong enough to continue the work.
evidence: seat of power is Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry
Beacon is Lock House Beacon inside Canal / Riverworks
Civic Services and Relief Hall are explicit district anchors
Medical / Healing and Arena / Training are full civic districts
Book 17 ties TVE to the Logistician and replacement/perseverance themes

districtCharacter.section1CanalRiverworks: the hydraulic foundation of the city: lock houses, sluice gates, water gauges, waterwheel yards, towpaths, wet stone, canal reflections, and the Lock House Beacon as public signal anchor
districtCharacter.section2GranaryFoodMarket: food security and public provisioning: grain halls, covered food markets, red awnings, grain scales, bakeries, bread ovens, storage jars, mill court, and giant-scale market logistics
districtCharacter.section3CivicServices: administrative relief: civic services hall, relief hall, bread tokens, water basins, blue service lamps, notice plazas, public rosters, and institutional response to need
districtCharacter.section4MedicalHealing: recovery infrastructure: healing hall, apothecary court, hospital halls, recovery beds, water bowls, herb gardens, bone-white ceremony, and public medical care
districtCharacter.section5ArenaTraining: replacement and disciplined strength: training arena, champion gate, sand floors, weapon courts, practice rings, victory stones, woven banners, and arena strength charms

streets.description: Tormund Vale streets should feel enormous, wet, measured, and purposeful. Characters walk along towpaths, lock walls, reed pavilion markets, grain halls, relief plazas, healing courtyards, and arena approaches sized for giants and organized around water, food, care, and replacement labor.
writingGuidance: Use giant scale through civic function: water gauges taller than people, grain scales like platforms, bread-token boards, vast basins, and doorways built for huge bodies.
Keep floodplain logistics visible in every district: sluices, raised paths, reed pavilions, water marks, storage jars, public rosters, and ward sigils.
Do not write TVE as brute-force giant culture; its strength is organized around provision, recovery, and continuity.
Use the river as a managed threat and managed resource, not a scenic backdrop.

people.description: Citizens should read as giants moving through infrastructure built to their scale: lock workers, millers, roster clerks, healers, apothecaries, relief officers, arena trainers, apprentices, haulers, and families living around systems too large for human assumptions.
behavioralNotes: giants should feel patient, procedural, and used to enormous shared tasks
status comes from responsibility: who keeps water flowing, food counted, rosters accurate, wounds treated, and replacements trained
citizens understand flood marks, grain tallies, relief categories, and arena ranks as ordinary civic language
the city should feel physically powerful but morally centered on carrying burdens after someone else can no longer carry them

shops.writingGuidance: Commerce should feel logistical and public-service adjacent. Even market activity should connect to grain, water, relief, healing, training, or the maintenance of floodplain life.

homes.description: Homes should be large riverstone, timber, mudbrick, limewash, reed, and canvas structures raised or terraced against floodplain risk, with broad thresholds, high sleeping platforms, shared basins, storage lofts, and giant-scale domestic tools.
writingGuidance: Make domestic life practical: water marks on walls, raised beds, grain jars, drying canvas, big benches, woven partitions, and household gauges.
Let ordinary giant homes show preparation for seasonal flood and communal replacement duties.

dailyRhythms: morning water gauge checks and lock-house reports
grain halls opening and bread-token boards being updated
relief hall rosters posted under blue service lamps
healing halls moving patients through gardens and water-basin courts
arena training sessions preparing replacements and emergency haulers
evening lanterns reflecting in canals and flood channels

sounds: canvas awnings snapping in floodplain wind

lighting.day: Daylight should reflect from canals, white limestone, wet stone, and limewashed civic walls, softened by reed pavilions and floodplain haze.
lighting.night: Night should be lantern-lit and blue-sigil marked, with canal reflections, service lamps, healing lamps, and arena torches showing the city still working after dark.
lighting.weather: Floodplain weather matters: rain, rising water, haze, humidity, and river fog should change routes, work rhythms, and emergency readiness.

transportation.description: Movement is by towpaths, raised river roads, lock bridges, canal edges, reed-covered walkways, giant-scale stairs, waterwheel yards, and broad routes between granary, relief, healing, and arena districts.
writingGuidance: Use giant stride and giant scale to alter scene pacing; crossing a plaza may be trivial for locals and exhausting for smaller visitors.
Let flood routing and lock operations delay or redirect movement naturally.
Use rosters, beacon states, and water levels to determine where crowds move.

children.description: Giant children should be visible near bread halls, waterwheel courts, healing gardens, and training yards, learning flood marks, food duties, roster etiquette, and how to move safely around machinery built for even larger adults.

craftsmen.description: Craftsmen include lock engineers, waterwheelwrights, millers, grain weighers, reed pavilion builders, roster copyists, ward-sigil masons, apothecaries, healing lamp keepers, arena trainers, and practice weapon makers.

markets.description: Markets should feel large, covered, and provision-focused: grain, bread, water equipment, reed construction, herbs, canvas, tow ropes, healing supplies, and arena gear.

addie: notices repeated roster categories, bread-token language, water-level marks, registry phrases, and replacement protocols
stores routes by Lock House Beacon, Grain Hall, Relief Hall, Healing Hall, Training Arena, Memory Tablet Registry, and Sky-Stair Hall
would recognize that TVE turns memory into logistics: names matter because someone must replace what was lost
gregory: reads floodgates, grain scales, blue order sigils, strength charms, memory tablets, and sky stairs as symbols of burden, provision, continuity, and ascent after loss
would distinguish giant strength as duty from giant strength as spectacle
brickett: spots minor gauge errors, loose sluice pins, roster edits, bread-token discrepancies, waterwheel wear, and service routes beneath huge structures
would be valuable because tiny overlooked failures can disrupt enormous civic systems
talandra: reads lock houses, towpaths, wet bridges, waterwheel yards, arena gates, and relief crowds as threat geometry
would worry about sabotage to water control more than direct combat
mae: would see leverage in grain access, water control, roster priority, healing eligibility, arena rank, and memory-tablet recordkeeping
would understand that in Tormund Vale, logistics is politics
rhun: should feel the Book 17 pressure here: replacement is not abstract, it is built into rosters, training yards, relief halls, and the city’s floodplain survival systems
would see the Black Tortoise Shell theme mirrored by a city that survives because someone always steps into the gap
genericCompanionNotes: Outsiders should feel physically small but also administratively small: the city is built around duties larger than one person.
TVE should make perseverance feel practical, recorded, and collectively assigned.

arrivalExperience.firstImpression: A visitor should first experience Tormund Vale as a floodplain city of giant-scale order: lock houses, sluice gates, white civic blocks, reed pavilions, grain halls, blue service lamps, huge public rosters, and distant sky-stairs that make survival feel like a task handed down from generation to generation.
firstHourSequence: approach along river or raised floodplain road
first sight of the Lock House Beacon and working sluice gates
crossing towpaths and waterwheel yards scaled for giants
passing grain halls, bread tokens, and covered food markets
moving through relief and healing districts where public care is procedural
hearing arena drills and seeing the Sky-Stair Hall / Memory Tablet Registry as symbols of replacement and perseverance
arrivalExperience.readerEffect: The reader should feel that Tormund Vale is enormous but not careless. Its scale exists to manage burdens too heavy for smaller civic systems: flood, hunger, illness, memory, and replacement.

readerShouldRemember: Lock House Beacon over wet stone and sluice gates
giant water gauges and waterwheel yards
grain halls, bread tokens, and covered food markets
blue service lamps and public rosters
Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud
a city where logistics is moral duty

wonder.name: Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud
wonder.purpose: The Wonder embodies Tormund Vale's highest civilizational idea: burdens can be climbed, inherited, and continued by those large enough and prepared enough to carry them.
wonder.visualDescription: A far skyline anchor of giant-scale stairs, halls, cloud-breaking terraces, riverstone, mudbrick, timber, limewash, canvas, ward networks, ritual geometry, and stone giant memory.
wonder.narrativeUse: Use the Wonder to make perseverance visible. It should feel less like triumph and more like ascent after rupture: the broken cloud is the gap, and the stairs are the civic answer.

howCharactersNoticeIt.addie: notices repeated ascent, burden, memory, and replacement phrases attached to civic speech
howCharactersNoticeIt.gregory: reads sky stairs and broken cloud as symbols of continuation after interruption
howCharactersNoticeIt.brickett: spots maintenance stairs, patched canvas, and small workers keeping the giant monument usable
howCharactersNoticeIt.talandra: maps exposure, choke points, and emergency evacuation along huge terraces
howCharactersNoticeIt.rhun: reads it as a personal accusation: someone must climb when the first bearer is gone

wonder.visibilityFromCity: Visible from hero views as the far skyline anchor and symbolically linked to registry, training, and floodplain endurance.

beacon.name: Lock House Beacon
beacon.purpose: The Beacon ties Conjunction signal identity to water control, flood warning, and the city's first line of public coordination.
beacon.visualDescription: A major lock-house signal landmark among wet stone, sluice gates, waterwheel yards, towpaths, white limestone civic blocks, reed pavilions, coral terraces, lantern light, and canal reflections.
beacon.narrativeUse: Use the Beacon as a floodplain warning instrument. A color change should move lock workers, grain halls, relief rosters, healing teams, and arena replacements at once.

howCharactersNoticeIt.addie: notices citizens pair beacon colors with water procedures and roster categories
howCharactersNoticeIt.gregory: reads it as a threshold between ordered water and disaster
howCharactersNoticeIt.brickett: finds ladder access, gauge errors, sluice pins, and hidden service points around it
howCharactersNoticeIt.talandra: treats it as critical infrastructure vulnerable to sabotage
howCharactersNoticeIt.mae: understands that control of the lock beacon is control of the city's emergency tempo

beacon.visibilityFromCity: Prominent from Section 1 and hero views as the near/front civic signal anchor.

seatOfPower.name: Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry
seatOfPower.purpose: The seat of power expresses governance through durable memory, public registration, sealed archives, command terraces, and stone giant continuity.
seatOfPower.visualDescription: A central civic authority of memory tablet walls, courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, riverstone, mudbrick, timber, limewash, and ward-network geometry scaled for giants.
seatOfPower.narrativeUse: Use the Registry to show that Tormund Vale governs by remembering who carries what duty. Names, rosters, replacements, water obligations, food obligations, and healing obligations all become civic memory.

howCharactersNoticeIt.addie: locks onto names, duty records, replacement categories, and archival omissions
howCharactersNoticeIt.gregory: reads memory tablets as civic immortality through responsibility
howCharactersNoticeIt.brickett: spots replaced tablets, repair seams, erased rosters, and clerical labor beneath the monumental record
howCharactersNoticeIt.talandra: maps public terraces, archive approaches, crowd movement, and defensive sightlines
howCharactersNoticeIt.mae: understands that who gets recorded as capable or replaceable gains or loses power

seatOfPower.visibilityFromCity: Dominant central civic anchor visible across hero views as the political and archival heart.

majorLandmarks.name: Lock House Beacon
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for flood warnings, water-control politics, signal anxiety, and emergency coordination.

majorLandmarks.name: Grain Hall
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for food security, market logistics, grain scales, bread tokens, and the moral politics of provision.

majorLandmarks.name: Relief Hall
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for civic services, public rosters, water basins, aid eligibility, and replacement after crisis.

majorLandmarks.name: Healing Hall
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for recovery, clinics, herb gardens, giant-scale medicine, and care for those who can no longer carry duty.

majorLandmarks.name: Training Arena
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for replacements, practice rings, champion gates, disciplined strength, and the social pressure to become capable.

monuments.name: Canal Reflections Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use to show water memory and the city's dependence on measured river behavior.

monuments.name: Awning Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use to anchor food-market identity, public provision, wheat sheaves, bread ovens, and storage jars.

monuments.name: Civic Services Lantern Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use to show public service as visible, reassuring, and procedural.

monuments.name: Medical Healing Lantern Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use to mark recovery as civic memory and not private weakness.

monuments.name: Woven Banners Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use for arena rank, district belonging, giant pride, and public training culture.

minorLandmarks.name: Waterwheel Court Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for small mechanical clues, floodplain labor, gauge readings, and Brickett-style overlooked failures.

minorLandmarks.name: Mill Court Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for bakeries, grain scales, covered food halls, and food-supply gossip.

minorLandmarks.name: Notice Plaza Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for public rosters, bread tokens, missing replacements, relief rules, and Addie's exact-memory comparisons.

minorLandmarks.name: Apothecary Court Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for herbs, white cloth, water bowls, healing lamps, and recovery scenes.

minorLandmarks.name: Champion Gate Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for training pressure, public tests, practice-ring entry, and replacement status.

immediateNarrativeImprovements.recommendation: Use TVE as the city where replacement is logistical, moral, and public: rosters, training yards, relief halls, healing halls, and memory tablets all force the question of who steps in when someone is gone.
Use lock houses, sluice gates, water gauges, waterwheel yards, grain scales, bread tokens, blue service lamps, public rosters, healing gardens, and champion gates as recurring texture.
Make the Lock House Beacon a floodplain coordination signal and the Sky-Stair Hall the emotional skyline for perseverance after rupture.

Let Addie parse rosters and records, Gregory read burden/ascent symbolism, Brickett catch tiny infrastructure failures, Talandra defend critical waterworks, Mae read logistics as power, and Rhun feel replacement as civic pressure.
Distinguish TVE from CPE: both are giant/colossus-scale, but CPE is monumental memory and public inscription while TVE is floodplain logistics, water control, provision, recovery, and replacement.

interpretation.civilizationThesis: Tormund Vale is the architecture of civic replacement: a giant floodplain civilization that survives because water, food, relief, healing, memory, and training are all organized to ensure someone can carry the next burden.
interpretation.environmentalPhilosophy: Measure and continue: the river will rise, people will fall, duties will break, and the city survives by measuring everything and preparing successors.
interpretation.adaptiveStrategy: Roster the burden: manage water, feed the city, heal the wounded, record duty, train replacements, and preserve memory through giant-scale institutions.
interpretation.relationshipWithNature: managerial and flood-aware; river and floodplain are permanent pressures answered through locks, gauges, canals, and public works
interpretation.relationshipWithHistory: tablet-based and duty-based; memory matters because it assigns continuity after loss
interpretation.relationshipWithKnowledge: procedural and practical; knowledge lives in water marks, grain tallies, rosters, healing protocols, and training records
interpretation.relationshipWithRisk: risk is managed through locks, grain stores, relief halls, healing systems, ward networks, and trained replacements
interpretation.relationshipWithBeauty: beauty is scale made useful: reflected canals, reed pavilions, white civic blocks, giant halls, blue service lamps, and sky stairs
interpretation.relationshipWithCommunity: community is burden-sharing at giant scale, with public systems ensuring flood, hunger, injury, and death do not stop the city

civilizationalTension.tension: person as irreplaceable vs office as replaceable
civilizationalTension.resolution: Tormund Vale resolves the tension imperfectly by honoring names on memory tablets while training someone else to carry the work.

interpretation.religiousExpression: civic-memory and ward-network oriented; ritual geometry, stone giant memory, arena strength charms, and sky-stair symbolism carry sacred-adjacent weight
interpretation.economicIdentity: waterworks, grain, bread tokens, relief services, healing, training, reed construction, canvas, ward maintenance, and giant-scale logistics
interpretation.socialIdentity: procedural, burden-conscious, provision-minded, physically immense, recovery-oriented, and emotionally shaped by succession

lockedCanon.statement: Tormund Vale is a mature giant floodplain city where canal/riverworks, granary markets, civic services, healing halls, training arenas, memory-tablet government, and the Sky-Stair Hall define civic identity.
lockedCanon.reason: The sidecars consistently identify TVE as a giant city using Floodplain/River context with Canal / Riverworks, Granary / Food Market, Civic Services, Medical / Healing, Arena / Training, Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, Lock House Beacon, and Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud.
supportingEvidence: Giant Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud

lockedCanon.statement: Tormund Vale's giant identity should be expressed through logistics and civic scale rather than simple brute force.
lockedCanon.reason: The district anchors emphasize water gauges, sluice gates, grain scales, bread tokens, public rosters, healing halls, and training yards.

lockedCanon.statement: TVE is a strong Book 17 replacement/perseverance city because its civic systems all prepare the city to continue after loss or crisis.
lockedCanon.reason: Book 17 associates TVE with the Logistician, replacement, Black Tortoise Shell, and perseverance, and the city’s imagery reinforces rosters, relief, healing, and training.

provisionalCanon.statement: Tormund Vale's highest civic virtue is perseverance.
provisionalCanon.reason: The city repeatedly turns flood, hunger, injury, duty, and replacement into civic systems, but the virtue language should be tested against the full Book 17 city set.

provisionalCanon.statement: Tormund Vale may carry a painful civic assumption that everyone can be replaced, even while memory tablets preserve individual names.
provisionalCanon.reason: The city’s replacement/training/roster systems point in this direction, but narrative scenes should decide how cruel or compassionate that assumption becomes.

workingHypotheses.statement: Giant civilizations may frame strength as logistical capacity: the ability to hold water, food, memory, and replacement systems at a scale smaller societies cannot manage.
workingHypotheses.reason: TVE strongly supports this, but comparison with CPE and other giant-scale cities is needed.

rejectedHypotheses.statement: Tormund Vale is just another monumental colossus city like CPE.
rejectedHypotheses.reason: CPE centers scale-as-memory and monumental judgment; TVE centers floodplain logistics, public provision, care, training, and replacement systems.

rejectedHypotheses.statement: Tormund Vale's waterworks are decorative river-town scenery.
rejectedHypotheses.reason: Section 1 makes lock houses, sluice gates, water gauges, waterwheel yards, and towpaths core civic anchors and Beacon identity.

futureQuestions: How explicitly should TVE's replacement culture pressure Rhun in Book 17?
Does the Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry honor replacement or erase the original bearer by converting them into a duty record?
Who controls bread-token and relief eligibility, and can logistics become oppressive?
How literal is the Giant Sky-Stair Hall's Broken Cloud symbolism in local myth?
How should TVE giant-body scale differ from CPE colossus monumentality in prose and future prompts?

distinctFrom.difference: CPE is monumental memory and public scale-as-judgment; TVE is floodplain logistics, provision, healing, training, and replacement.
DLS is river-lock beastfolk civic engineering; TVE is giant-scale floodplain logistics tied to memory, food, healing, and replacement.
HEA centers care infrastructure and repair; TVE centers broader logistical continuity where care is one part of replacement systems.
VLG institutionalizes winter endurance; TVE institutionalizes floodplain continuity and replacement readiness.

similarTo.similarity: Both use giant/colossus scale and memory tablet authority, but for different civic philosophies.
Both use water control infrastructure as a primary civic identity.
Both make public healing and aid visible as civic systems.

uniqueContributions: best city for Book 17 replacement pressure
distinct fusion of waterworks, grain, relief, healing, training, and memory tablets
potentialInfluences: Mother's observation that civilizations can turn replacement into public infrastructure

previousAssumptions: Tormund Vale was expected to be a giant city with floodplain/river identity.
Its exact Book 17 function around logistics, replacement, healing, and training had not yet been formalized.
discoveries: TVE is not just a giant city; it is a logistical continuity city.
The Lock House Beacon makes water control the public signal center.
Granary, relief, healing, and arena districts create a strong sequence from survival to recovery to replacement.
Memory Tablet Walls connect public remembrance with assignment of ongoing duties.
The Sky-Stair Hall of the Broken Cloud gives perseverance a strong visual and symbolic skyline.
confirmedAssumptions: Tormund Vale should feel giant-scaled, river/floodplain grounded, established, and civic rather than primitive.
Waterworks, grain logistics, and public service belong in its core visual grammar.
The city is directly useful for Book 17 replacement/perseverance themes.
changedOurMind: Do not lean on generic giant monumentality alone; TVE's distinctiveness is logistics, provision, healing, and replacement.
Make giant scale functional and civic rather than only spectacular.
Use rosters, gauges, bread tokens, recovery beds, and training yards as story-bearing details.
futureValidation: Compare TVE against CPE and other giant-scale cities to refine giant civilization differences.
Use Book 17 scenes to test whether replacement is compassionate, oppressive, or both.
Revisit TVE prose for enough lock-house, granary, relief, healing, and arena specificity.
